steps.title: Identify the unit conversions needed
steps.content:
To convert from m/s² to miles/min², I must convert both the numerator and denominator separately.

For the numerator: 1 mile = 1,609 meters, so I divide by 1,609.

For the denominator: 1 minute = 60 seconds, which means 1 minute² = 60² = 3,600 seconds².

This is the key step—when converting squared units, the conversion factor itself must be squared.

steps.title: Apply the conversion factors
steps.content: $$6.10 \text{ m/s}^2 \times \frac{1 \text{ mile}}{1,609 \text{ m}} \times \frac{3,600 \text{ s}^2}{1 \text{ min}^2}$$ The units cancel, leaving: $$6.10 \times \frac{3,600}{1,609}$$

steps.title: Calculate and round
steps.content: $$6.10 \times \frac{3,600}{1,609} = \frac{21,960}{1,609} \approx 13.648$$ Rounding to the nearest tenth: 13.6 miles per minute squared.
